name: Distributable Earnings per Share
calculation_notes: Distributable Earnings (DE) divided by diluted weighted-average shares outstanding. DE is typically reported as a non-GAAP measure representing cash earnings available for distribution, calculated as Fee-Related Earnings plus realized performance revenues minus realized performance compensation, plus realized investment income, minus taxes and other adjustments. Use the firm's reported DE figure where available; cross-check against FRE + realized carry + realized investment income - taxes.

name: Earnings per Share (GAAP)
calculation_notes: GAAP net income attributable to common shareholders divided by diluted weighted-average shares outstanding, as reported in SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q, or 20-F). For firms reporting under IFRS (e.g., Brookfield), use the IFRS basic/diluted EPS as reported. Include both continuing and discontinued operations unless the firm separately discloses continuing-only EPS as the primary figure.

name: DE/Share Growth 3-Year CAGR
calculation_notes: Compound annual growth rate of Distributable Earnings per share over the trailing three fiscal years. Calculated as (DE/share_t / DE/share_{t-3})^(1/3) - 1. If the base-year DE/share is negative or zero, the metric is marked as not calculable (null). Use fiscal-year-end figures for consistency across the peer universe.

name: Fee-Earning Assets Under Management
calculation_notes: Total assets under management on which the firm earns management fees. Reported directly by most alt-asset managers in earnings supplements. For firms that do not separately disclose FEAUM (rare), approximate as total AUM minus unfunded commitments minus AUM in fee-holiday periods. Express in billions USD. For non-USD reporters, convert at the period-end exchange rate and note the FX rate used.

name: Total Assets Under Management
calculation_notes: Total assets under management as reported by the firm, including fee-earning and non-fee-earning assets, unfunded commitments, and co-investment vehicles where the firm exercises investment discretion. Express in billions USD. Use the firm's own reported Total AUM figure; do not attempt to reconstruct from sub-fund disclosures.

name: FEAUM Year-over-Year Growth
calculation_notes: Year-over-year percentage change in Fee-Earning AUM. Calculated as (FEAUM_t / FEAUM_{t-1}) - 1, where t and t-1 are the same quarter one year apart (e.g., Q4 2025 vs Q4 2024). If the firm underwent a material acquisition during the period, note the organic vs. inorganic split where disclosed.

name: FEAUM 3-Year CAGR
calculation_notes: Compound annual growth rate of Fee-Earning AUM over the trailing three fiscal years. Calculated as (FEAUM_t / FEAUM_{t-3})^(1/3) - 1. Use fiscal-year-end FEAUM values. If the firm's reporting history is shorter than three years (e.g., post-IPO), mark as not calculable (null).

name: Net Organic FEAUM Growth
calculation_notes: FEAUM growth attributable to organic fundraising and net inflows, excluding the impact of acquisitions, market appreciation/depreciation, and FX effects. Calculated as (new subscriptions + commitments deployed - redemptions - realizations) / beginning-of-period FEAUM. Where firms do not explicitly disclose organic FEAUM growth, approximate by subtracting disclosed acquisition-related AUM additions and estimated market appreciation from total FEAUM change. Flag approximated values.

name: Fundraising as Percentage of AUM
calculation_notes: Total capital raised (gross inflows) during the trailing twelve months divided by beginning-of-period Total AUM. Includes fund closes, separate account mandates, and co-investment commitments. Excludes leverage and recycled capital. Use gross fundraising as reported in earnings supplements or annual reports. Express as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 for 15%).

name: Asset Class Herfindahl-Hirschman Index
calculation_notes: Herfindahl-Hirschman Index measuring concentration across asset classes. Calculated as sum of squared market share proportions × 10,000, where each asset class's share is its AUM divided by Total AUM. Higher HHI indicates greater concentration (less diversification). Standard asset class buckets: Private Equity, Credit, Real Assets/Infrastructure, Real Estate, Hedge Fund Solutions, Insurance Solutions, Public Markets/Other. Map each firm's reported segments to these buckets. HHI = 10000 means single asset class; HHI approaching 10000/N (where N = number of classes) indicates equal diversification.

name: Permanent Capital as Percentage of AUM
calculation_notes: AUM in perpetual or permanent capital vehicles divided by Total AUM. Permanent capital includes: publicly listed vehicles, evergreen funds, CLOs (post-reinvestment period), insurance float, and other structures with no contractual redemption or termination date. Some firms report this directly; for others, sum AUM from identified permanent capital vehicles. Express as a decimal (e.g., 0.40 for 40%).

name: Credit as Percentage of AUM
calculation_notes: AUM in credit strategies (including direct lending, distressed, structured credit, CLOs, mezzanine, and liquid credit) divided by Total AUM. Use the firm's own credit segment definition where available. For firms with blended segments (e.g., 'Credit & Insurance'), use the credit sub-segment if disclosed, otherwise use the full blended segment and flag. Express as a decimal.

name: Fee-Related Earnings Margin
calculation_notes: Fee-Related Earnings divided by total management fee revenue (including transaction and monitoring fees where reported as part of fee revenue). FRE is the non-GAAP measure of recurring, fee-based profitability before realized performance revenues. Use the firm's reported FRE and fee revenue figures. Express as a decimal (e.g., 0.55 for 55% margin). For firms that report FRE but define fee revenue differently, ensure the denominator matches the revenue base used in the FRE calculation.

name: Fee-Related Earnings Growth Year-over-Year
calculation_notes: Year-over-year percentage change in Fee-Related Earnings. Calculated as (FRE_t / FRE_{t-1}) - 1, comparing the same period one year apart (LTM or fiscal year). If the base-period FRE is negative, mark as not calculable (null). Express as a decimal.

name: Compensation-to-Revenue Ratio
calculation_notes: Total compensation and benefits expense divided by total revenues (GAAP or segment-level, depending on firm reporting). Includes base compensation, bonus accruals, equity-based compensation, and benefits. Excludes realized performance compensation (which is netted against realized performance revenues in FRE/DE frameworks). For firms that report segment-level comp, use the segment figure that corresponds to the management company. Express as a decimal.

name: Management Fee Rate
calculation_notes: Effective management fee rate calculated as Fee-Related Earnings revenue base (management fees + transaction/monitoring fees) divided by average FEAUM for the period, expressed in basis points. Calculated as (fee revenue / average FEAUM) × 10,000. Use the average of beginning and ending FEAUM for the period. This captures the blended fee rate across all strategies and vintages. Higher rates generally indicate greater pricing power or a higher-fee strategy mix (e.g., PE vs. credit).

name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
calculation_notes: Realized performance revenues (carried interest + incentive fees) divided by total segment revenues. Use realized (not unrealized) performance revenues to align with cash-based earnings quality. For firms that report both realized and unrealized, use only the realized component. Express as a decimal. Higher values indicate greater earnings cyclicality and dependence on fund performance. LTM figures preferred to smooth quarterly volatility.

name: Headcount to FEAUM Ratio
calculation_notes: Total headcount (full-time employees and equivalents) divided by Fee-Earning AUM in billions USD. Measures labor intensity of the asset management platform. Lower values indicate greater operational scalability. Headcount sourced from 10-K/20-F employee disclosures, proxy statements, or earnings supplements. If only year-end headcount is available, use that figure against year-end FEAUM for consistency. Express as employees per billion USD FEAUM.

name: FEAUM per Employee
calculation_notes: Fee-Earning AUM divided by total headcount. Inverse of MET-VD-018. Expressed in millions USD per employee. Higher values indicate greater asset-gathering efficiency per person. Use the same headcount and FEAUM figures as MET-VD-018 for consistency.

name: Compensation and Benefits to FEAUM
calculation_notes: Total compensation and benefits expense divided by average FEAUM, expressed in basis points. Calculated as (total comp & benefits / average FEAUM) × 10,000. Includes base salary, bonuses, equity-based compensation, and benefits. If comp is reported only at the segment level, use the management-company segment. This metric captures the all-in labor cost of managing fee-earning assets. Lower values indicate greater compensation efficiency relative to the asset base.

name: General and Administrative Expense to FEAUM
calculation_notes: General and administrative expense divided by average FEAUM, expressed in basis points. Calculated as (G&A expense / average FEAUM) × 10,000. G&A includes occupancy, technology, professional fees, travel, insurance, and other overhead. Excludes compensation (captured in MET-VD-020) and fund-level expenses passed through to LPs. Lower values indicate leaner overhead structure relative to the fee-earning asset base.

name: Operating Expense Growth Minus Fee Revenue Growth
calculation_notes: Trailing twelve-month operating expense growth rate minus trailing twelve-month fee revenue growth rate. A negative value indicates positive operating leverage (expenses growing slower than revenue); a positive value indicates dis-economies. Calculated as OpEx_growth_YoY - FeeRevenue_growth_YoY. Both growth rates use the same period definition (LTM or fiscal year). Express as a decimal spread (e.g., -0.05 means OpEx grew 5 percentage points slower than fee revenue).

name: Constant Currency Revenue Growth
calculation_notes: Revenue growth rate after removing the impact of foreign exchange translation effects. If the firm discloses constant-currency revenue growth directly, use that figure. Otherwise, approximate by re-translating prior-period non-USD revenues at current-period exchange rates. This metric is particularly relevant for firms with significant non-USD AUM (e.g., PAX with BRL-denominated funds, Brookfield with CAD/AUD exposure). Express as a decimal. If a firm operates entirely in USD with no material FX exposure, this metric equals reported revenue growth.

name: Integration Costs to Revenue
calculation_notes: Separately disclosed integration and restructuring costs divided by total revenue. Only populated when the firm explicitly discloses integration costs as a line item or in footnotes (typically following acquisitions). If not separately disclosed, set to null rather than estimating. Express as a decimal. This metric captures the operational drag of M&A integration and is relevant for serial acquirers (e.g., Blue Owl, Brookfield). Expected to have < 60% coverage across the universe.

name: Capital Expenditure to FEAUM
calculation_notes: Technology and software capital expenditure divided by average FEAUM, expressed in basis points. Only populated when the firm separately identifies technology/software capex in financial statements or supplements (as distinct from fund-level capital deployment). Most alt-asset managers are asset-light and do not separately disclose platform-level capex. If not separately identifiable, set to null. Expected to have < 60% coverage across the universe.

name: Price to Fee-Related Earnings
calculation_notes: Market capitalization (or share price) divided by Fee-Related Earnings (or FRE per share). Use LTM FRE for consistency. Market cap based on diluted share count × closing price at measurement date. P/FRE is the primary valuation multiple for alt-asset managers because FRE captures the recurring, management-fee-based earnings stream that the market values most highly. Exclude firms with negative LTM FRE from this multiple.

name: Price to Distributable Earnings
calculation_notes: Market capitalization (or share price) divided by Distributable Earnings (or DE per share). Use LTM DE for consistency. P/DE captures total cash-generative capacity including realized performance revenues and investment income. More volatile than P/FRE due to performance fee cyclicality. Exclude firms with negative LTM DE from this multiple.

name: Enterprise Value to Fee-Earning AUM
calculation_notes: Enterprise value divided by Fee-Earning AUM. Enterprise value = market capitalization + total debt - cash and equivalents (using the management company balance sheet, not fund-level debt). Express as a percentage (e.g., 0.03 means the market values each dollar of FEAUM at 3 cents of EV). This multiple directly measures how much the market pays per unit of fee-earning asset base. Useful for cross-firm comparison regardless of capital structure or earnings methodology differences.

name: Average Daily Trading Volume
calculation_notes: Average number of shares traded per day over the trailing 90 calendar days (approximately 63 trading days). Sourced from exchange data or financial data providers. Used as a liquidity indicator to contextualize valuation multiples — lower ADTV may indicate a liquidity discount. Not included in correlation analysis as it is a market microstructure variable, not a fundamental driver.

name: Passive Ownership Percentage
calculation_notes: Percentage of outstanding shares held by passive/index funds (ETFs and index mutual funds). Sourced from 13-F filings, proxy statements, or data providers (e.g., Bloomberg, FactSet). Higher passive ownership may indicate greater index inclusion effects on valuation. Express as a decimal (e.g., 0.25 for 25%). Not included in correlation analysis.

name: Free Float Percentage
calculation_notes: Percentage of outstanding shares available for public trading, excluding shares held by insiders, strategic holders, and restricted stock. Sourced from exchange filings or data providers. Lower free float may contribute to valuation premiums or discounts due to reduced liquidity. Express as a decimal (e.g., 0.60 for 60%). Not included in correlation analysis.
